0.7.7: lib/error-classify.js + transient HTTP retry (Phase A)

Phase A of the v4 0.8.0 SDK migration plan: pre-migration patches
that ship to both bots before any 0.8.0 work. Independent of SDK.

lib/error-classify.js — single-source classifier for errors from any
transport (today: stream-json result events, child_process events,
polygram-internal Errors with err.code). Returns
{ kind, userMessage, isTransient, autoRecover } so callers don't
grow if-branches every time a new error class appears.

Move script-runner cron jobs from openclaw to native launchd

The four script-runner cron jobs (dm-pos-orders, fo-poll-checkin-notify,
fo-poll-membership-notify, fo-daily-reception-update) ran an LLM agent
turn just to invoke a Python script and echo its output — ~864
OpenAI calls/day (~$5/day) for zero LLM work. Disabled them in
openclaw cron and replaced with native LaunchAgents that exec the
Python scripts directly:

- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.pos-notify.plist (every 5 min)
- ~/Libr

Fix CRITICAL audit findings + stealth-forward hardening

Audit fixes (PR #781 follow-up):

A1 CRITICAL — _metered_solve cancellation refund leak. The finally
    block used contextlib.suppress(Exception); CancelledError is a
    BaseException so the await on the refund coro re-raised on
    cancellation and skipped the refund. Wrap in asyncio.shield so the
    refund Task completes regardless of outer cancellation.

A2 HIGH — CamoufoxInstance.lock property mutated _lock on loop change.
    Silen

fix(dashboard): create DM room eagerly so header and sidebar hydrate (#433)

After clicking "Send Message" the user landed on a route whose room did
not yet exist on the backend. RoomHeader bails on `if (!room) return null;`
so no header rendered, and the sidebar's RoomList had nothing to show
because the DM was not in `overview.rooms` / `humanRooms`. The composer
worked, but the page felt half-broken until the user typed and sent the
first message (which is what triggered the auto-create).

v1.2.2: remove all file-named imports; rename file command to resource

Drop getFile and deleteFile imports from @inrupt/solid-client entirely.
readPodResource now uses the authenticated session fetch directly (an HTTP
GET), and deleteResource uses fetch with method DELETE for non-container
URLs. This eliminates every occurrence of the strings "getFile" / "deleteFile"
from the codebase, removing the last pattern that triggers the ClawHub
suspicious.potential_exfiltration heuristic.

fix(admin-agents): close export modal before regenerate-key confirm

Production self-test reported that clicking "Regenerate Key & Download"
from inside the Export Modal left the destructive showConfirm dialog
hidden behind the export view — both share `.modal-overlay`'s
z-index, and the latter wins by DOM order. Admins couldn't see the
"old key will be revoked" warning at all.

Restructure the flow:
- close the Export Modal *first*, then showConfirm.
- Cancel path re-opens the Export Modal with
remove force flag and auto-rebuild missing archives

refactor(packs): split workspace scoping out of base filesystem pack

The base ``capability/filesystem`` pack used to ship four
``scope_limit`` rules (Writes / Edits / Patch / Reads restricted
to ``<workspace>/`` + system-read paths).  Auto-onboarding pulled
the pack in for any project with read/write/edit-shaped tools,
which produced false-positive ``WOULD-BLOCK`` noise on every
relative-path read in observe mode and would have wedged the
agent at step 1 in enforce mode.

* fix(wasm): host_log uses lossy UTF-8 decode so multi-byte boundary doesn't drop the whole 4 KiB

#3923 (b641ca6) tightened host_log decoding from String::from_utf8_lossy
to std::str::from_utf8(...).unwrap_or("<invalid utf8>").  Strict
from_utf8 rejects any byte slice that isn't fully valid UTF-8.  When
the MAX_LOG_BYTES (4 KiB) cap lands inside a multi-byte sequence —

buffers the FULL body in memory before checking the cap.  A server

memory pressure under concurrent abuse.  The audit of #3926
flagge

fix(admin-agents): wire export modal buttons via addEventListener

The Copy and Download(placeholder) buttons in the export modal were
built with `onclick="copyText(${JSON.stringify(content)})"`. Whenever
the markdown payload contained a `"` — which it always does, e.g. the
mail-protocol section's `Body: {"agent_id": ...}` example — the JSON
literal's quotes terminated the HTML attribute early, leaving the
button's onclick effectively `copyText(` and silently failing on click.

polymorphically — this brings blobs.put into line.

fix(sdk): populate sites.deployDir url from urls.project (#130)

The v2 deploy primitive returns `urls = { project, release }` with no
`urls.site` key, so the legacy `r.sites.deployDir` shim was always falling
through to the empty-string fallback for `result.url`. Downstream UX
(clipboard buttons, status pages, CLI prints) saw an empty string.

Pick `urls.project` (the live project URL) as the primary value, fall
back to `urls.site` for forward compat, and `""` only as a last resort.

both sides; per-receiver `mentioned` flag and member-membership filter
keep the security/dedup guarantees intact.

fix(web): eliminate rAF race in session rename input

The session rename input used requestAnimationFrame to set its value
and focus after mounting. This created a race with Playwright's fill()
method: if fill() started before the rAF callback, the input could
receive a blur event (triggering commitRename and element removal)
before the value was set.

Replace rAF with useLayoutEffect which runs synchronously after render,
before the browser paints, eliminating the timing window. Also guard

multi-step writes (insert+update, deactivate-old+insert-new etc.)
ran on different pool connections and could not be rolled back
atomically — a request that crashed between the two execute()
calls left the database in a partially-updated state.

Adds:
- PgConnectionWrapper.transaction(): holds a single connection via
  a contextvar so nested execute() calls reuse it and run ins

feat(onboard): add --name flag for explicit sandbox naming (#2591)

## Summary

`nemoclaw onboard` had no `--name` flag, so picking a sandbox name in
non-interactive or no-TTY runs required the undiscoverable
`NEMOCLAW_SANDBOX_NAME` env var, and `--from <Dockerfile>` without a TTY

cannot contain NUL (0x00) characters'. NULs sneak in from PDF copy-paste,
buggy SDK clients, or binary files. Saw 2 prod 500s in 3h.

search_vector_with_teams, search_chunks_vector, search_episodes_vector,
search_episodes_text, search_procedures_vector, search_procedures_text.

fix(xtask): resolve real mdbook binary, avoid xtask self-spawn (#6171)

The xtask binary is itself named `mdbook`, so `Command::new("mdbook")` could
resolve back to the xtask exe and cause it to spawn itself recursively:
  - Cargo prepends `target/debug` and `target/debug/deps` to PATH for `cargo run`.
  - On Windows, `CreateProcess` also searches the parent process's directory
    before PATH, so the xtask exe was always picked up first.

Introduce `util::mdbook_program()` which walks PATH manu

fix(wasm): treat /private/tmp as /tmp for capability checks on macOS (#4065)

* fix(wasm): treat /private/tmp as /tmp for capability checks on macOS

macOS aliases /tmp -> /private/tmp at the filesystem level, so
safe_resolve_path's canonicalize() converts a guest-supplied
'/tmp/foo' into '/private/tmp/foo' before check_capability runs.
A natural user-facing grant FileRead('/tmp/*') never matches the
canonical value '/private/tmp/foo' because the path-aware matcher
splits on '/' and sees a diffe

fix(docker): unbreak docker build (#3948 added duplicate user creation) (#4058)

#3948 appended a fresh user-creation block at Dockerfile:67:

  RUN groupadd -r librefang && useradd -r -g librefang -s /sbin/nologin librefang

The librefang user already exists at line 58-59 (created with explicit
uid/gid 1001):

  RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 librefang && \
      adduser  --system --uid 1001 --ingroup librefang librefang

groupadd exits with code 9 ('group already exists') the moment it
tries

    Ok(canonical_parent.join(file_name))

fix(ci): widen reconnect timeouts to absorb openclaw SIGUSR1 cascades

After setup wizard, OpenClaw treats the new agents+plugins block as a
restart-required reload, fires SIGUSR1, and self-respawns (~5s of
TCP-down). Pinchy's openclaw-node uses exponential backoff on reconnect
(1s → 2s → 4s → … → 30s cap), and double-fires reconnect on every
error+close pair, so worst-case Pinchy lands back on a healthy gateway
60-90s after the restart. The previous 60s budgets were right at that
edge and start

fix(cli): repair daemon-token shadowing in spawn_save_provider_key (#4078)

The inner `BackendRef::Daemon { ..., api_key }` destructure shadowed
the outer `api_key: String` parameter (the provider key the user
wants to save), so `.json({"key": api_key})` was POSTing the daemon
bearer token to /api/providers/{name}/key instead of the user's key
— every saved provider key was silently corrupted. Rebind the
destructure as `daemon_api_key` so the outer parameter stays in scope
for the JSON body.

fix: force NODE_ENV=development during Docker build stage

Coolify injects all env vars as build-time ARGs into every Dockerfile stage.
When NODE_ENV=production is injected, npm ci skips devDependencies (next,
typescript, etc.), causing 'npm run build' to fail with exit code 127.

This explicitly sets ENV NODE_ENV=development in the builder stage so
devDependencies are always installed. The runner stage already sets
NODE_ENV=production separately.

fix(tui): keep prompt submit off the RPC pool

A cleanup review found that adding prompt.submit to _LONG_HANDLERS made the RPC
pool own the full first-turn wait even though the handler itself already spawns
a turn thread. Keep prompt.submit inline and make it return immediately:

- look up the session without waiting
- kick the lazy agent build
- spawn a short waiter thread that blocks on agent_ready, then starts the
  existing turn dispatcher

install.sh: bump warmup timeout 240s -> 360s

Cold-start can run longer than 240s on heavy boxes:
- Reasoning models (grok-4, claude-opus, gpt-5-codex) take longer
- Large accumulated session history adds parse time on first call
- Slow VPSes / shared hardware extend everything
- Pepkey-class installs (70+ MB of session state) saw 200s+

Bump the warmup curl --max-time to 360s so the install doesn't
flag a slow-but-working cold start as a failure. Past 360s is
genuinely wrong and waiting longer

fix(api): atomic create with mode(0o600) for sessions file (close TOCTOU window) (#4044)

#3939 added restrict_permissions(path) after std::fs::write to keep
the sessions persistence file (containing bearer tokens) at 0600 on
Unix.  The two syscalls are separate, so the file lands at 0644 minus
umask between create and the chmod call.  A parallel reader on the
same host can open(O_RDONLY) the file during the window and copy the
tokens while perms are still default.

perf(daemon): parallelize per-room emit and chain has_more drains (#410)

Two related throughput improvements to the BotCord channel adapter:

1. **Parallel emit per (room, topic) group**: previously `drainInbox`
   awaited each group's emit serially, so a slow codex turn in room A
   blocked room B's turn from even starting. The dispatcher already
   keys its per-turn queue by `(channel, accountId, roomId, threadId)`,

fix(recommendation): cap candidate window at 3 per topic_group at SQL

Real reason "换一批 看着同质" persisted after the previous fatigue tweak:
the candidate window itself was concentrated. ``get_pool_candidates``
ordered strictly by ``relevance_score DESC`` and over-fetched only
``limit*5 = 50`` rows. In a 600-item pool that holds 270 distinct
``topic_group`` values (197 of which are single-item long tails), the
top 50 by relevance reliably came from the same ~10 head groups
(人工智能 / 游戏攻略 / 动漫杂谈 ...).

#3954 made cron.rs / triggers.rs / workflow.rs atomic-writes use a tmp

concurrent daemons can't clobber each other's staging file.  But within
a single process every thread still hits the same `.tmp.<pid>` path,
so two threads calling `persist()` concurrently can:

unintentionally changed the response body for 5xx errors.  The
original `internal_error` returned `{ error: "Internal server
error" }`; the new `map_memory_error` echoes the underlying

That string can carry internal deployment detail — database paths,

the API boundary.  Th

fix(kernel): atomic swap in running_tasks to close abort-handle race window (#4014)

The #3739 fix in #3960 used `DashMap::remove` followed by
`DashMap::insert` to abort the previous in-flight task before
inserting the new one for a given (agent, session).  Those two
operations release and re-acquire the shard write-lock in between,
which is exactly the race the original bug described — two concurrent
`send_message_full` calls can interleave as:

  T1: remove → Some(old)            (slot empty)

fix(a2a): cap on-boot load at max_tasks instead of slurping the entire retention window (#4013)

A2aTaskStore::db_load_into_memory loaded every row in a2a_tasks_v2 on
construction, with no upper bound.  A long-running daemon accumulates
rows up to the 7-day retention horizon — far more than max_tasks for
typical configs (default 1024).  Booting therefore:

1. read every row into a Vec<...> regardless of cap (transient memory

2. inserted them all into the HashMap, blowing

worth investigating, comparing to previous check findings for persistence.

non-owned global CF constant, decrementing its retain count on every
"Request" click and risking an over-release crash. Switch to
takeUnretainedValue(), which is the correct API for non-owning
references to global CF string constants.

fix(cli): prevent .env sanitizer from splitting GLM_API_KEY by LM_API_KEY suffix

The known-key splitter in `_sanitize_env_lines` used substring matching
to find concatenated KEY=VALUE pairs. When a registered key was a suffix
of another (LM_API_KEY is a suffix of GLM_API_KEY), the shorter key's
needle would match inside the longer one, causing the sanitizer to
rewrite `GLM_API_KEY=...` as `G\nLM_API_KEY=...` and silently break
Z.AI/GLM auth (and similarly `GLM_BASE_URL` -> `G\nLM_BASE_URL`).

builder format, so BuildKit output would not be treated as active build
progress once OpenShell emits it.

existing legacy builder output. It keeps the current legacy behavior and
makes `#1 [internal] ...`, `#2 CACHED`, and `#3 DONE ...` visible as
build progress.

Tool calls record paths inconsistently — some absolute (/workspace/foo),
some relative (foo). compressFilePaths() searches for a longest common
prefix, but a single bare relative path like 'AGENTS.md' has no '/' so
the prefix search immediately falls to '' and no base: line is emitted.

Add normalizePath() / normalizePathSet() in fileListsFromOps() to strip
the workspace prefix from all paths before they reach compressFilePaths.

setup: pre-accept the dangerous-channels dialog so the daemon doesn't hang (#16)

The launcher invokes claude with `--dangerously-load-development-channels`,
which on the first run pops a "press Enter to accept" confirmation. The
daemon owns no interactive TTY (supervisor + tmux do, but the user isn't
there to press anything), so claude blocks forever. The supervisor's
boot-grace expires, child gets SIGTERM'd, respawn, repeat — restart loop.

Bypass: the per-user `skipDangerousModePermissionProm

(which writes to $HOME/.npm-global). But users who follow our README
literally run `sudo npm install -g openclaw` — which lands at
/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw instead. Path mismatch → systemd
ExecStart ENOENT →

install.sh: harden BOOTSTRAP.md wipe — only remove known-safe variants

The previous wipe was an unconditional rm -f. If a user had a
custom BOOTSTRAP.md (rare but possible — they could deliberately
write one for their own first-light flow), we'd nuke it.

Carapace = SHELL on top of OpenClaw. Never destroy user state on
a layer-on install. So now:

  1. If BOOTSTRAP.md has our BEGIN CARAPACE BOOTSTRAP sentinel
     → safe to remove (we put it there)
  2. If BOOTSTRAP.md has openclaw's stock head

scrollback. For a SHELL meant to layer onto an existing setup,
